transcript: My name's Lucas Bruder, I'm the co-founder and CEO at Jito Labs. I'm here to talk about BAM today. BAM is programmable infrastructure for internet capital markets. Solana is winning the speed war. This is pretty clear. We've seen a 6x increase in transactions per second over the last few years. This is thanks to the hard work of all the Solana core engineers from Anza, Firedancer, and all the great apps on Solana. On the uh on the right here, you can see the blockspace increase over the past few years on Solana. Beginning of this year we were around 48 million compute units. Went to 50 million compute units, 60 million compute units. I think early next year we'll have 100 million compute units. Past then, I think it's going to continue to go exponential. Speed alone isn't enough though. Markets need more. There's a few properties we think markets need. They need to be transparent. People need to know the rules of the market and how the market works. They need to be fair. This is very important. Same rules, consistently applied. It needs to be verifiable. Prove it, don't just promise it. They also need to be customizable. We think that different markets have different needs. We think that BAM can help solve some of these issues. BAM is a transparent, verifiable, and customizable scheduler for Solana. There's two main pieces for BAM. There's the BAM node, which schedules transactions inside of trusted execution environments. They produce cryptographic attestations so you can get the verifiability that I mentioned on the last slide. And BAM validators will execute those transactions. The really cool thing about BAM is plugins. I mentioned different markets, different needs. BAM will support customized sequencing rules for those applications on Solana. This is commonly known as ACE or Application-Controlled Execution. And we're also exploring a lot of interesting and unique transaction primitives that we think will upgrade Solana. There's a few key properties for BAM that we care a lot about and that provide a lot of value to Solana. Talked about plugins, custom sequencing logic, applications can define their own markets through ACE. It's provable. You can know how it works, you can get attestations from the system to understand the software that's running, understand the market. And anyone can verify the code that's running is what it says it is. It's private. Transactions are encrypted through secure hardware on the trusted execution environment. It's also very performant. Today we're running around seven BAM nodes. We envision tens or hundreds run by many operators. The performance overhead from BAM is very small and we get all of these properties thanks to TEEs. BAM's a sandbox for innovation. Mentioned Application-Controlled Execution. This allows on-chain applications to implement their own ordering rules, create their own market microstructure. Common example here is maker priority. What this allows applications to do is provide better markets for makers. Ultimately this results in better prices for users. ACE allows the market microstructure to become fully programmable. And we think that when any asset can be traded on Solana, any market and application can define its own rules, then Solana will become the best venue to trade any asset, including the securities that Michael was just talking about. Today, BAM is running on 8% of stake. It's processed tens of thousands of blocks over the past few months and hundreds of millions of transactions. We see performance comparable to other validator clients on the network. So, where are we going with BAM? Talked a lot about plugins, Application-Controlled Execution. This is something that we're going to start working on very shortly. It's the most requested feature from applications on Solana, from prop AMMs to perp DEXs and other trading intense venues. I mentioned verifiability. We're working on something that we internally call BARS. This is the BAM Attestation Report Service. This lets you understand the market and the software that's running inside of BAM so traders can understand how the system works. A very important part of BAM is open sourcing the software. This is something that we're working on. We expect it to be open sourced within a few months. Decentralization is extremely important. And we'll be working on supporting multiple operators to onboard to BAM and run it across the world. Finally, we'll be working on IBRL. So this includes faster software, better performance, FireBAM which is our Firedancer implementation of BAM as well. There's a lot of work to do. Job's not finished. If any of this interests you, if you're a validator, you can scan the QR code, take you to bam.dev to learn more. If you're an application developer, please scan that, reach out to us. Happy to learn more about your needs, what you want to see on the market. And if you're an engineer and any of this interests you, if you want to work on very high performance systems, you want to help make Solana the best place it can be, the other QR code takes us to our job page. Hiring a lot of engineering right now. So, please apply. That's all I have for today. Thank you.

sources.title: How BAM extends Solana’s Lead: Making Internet Capital Markets A Reality | BAM
sources.snippet:
[Back to Blog](https://bam.dev/blog/)

November 12, 2025 • 8 minute read

# How BAM extends Solana’s Lead: Making Internet Capital Markets A Reality

Lucas Bruder

Jito Labs

Share

- _Solana's long-term success depends on deterministic execution infrastructure that scales transparently as the network grows. BAM exists to ensure that as Solana scales, the execution layer remains transparent, fair, and aligned with the applications and users who drive network growth._
- _BAM provides cryptographically verifiable transparency for sequencing that no other solution can provide: every ordering decision proven, not promised._
- _BAM will help everyone using or building on Solana succeed:_
 - _Applications gain the execution certainty needed to offer tighter spreads and predictable fills through Application Controlled Execution (ACE)._
 - _Traders benefit from superior execution quality that rivals or exceeds other venues._
 - _Validators get open-source infrastructure they can verify. When applications succeed on Solana, validators succeed._

# What Is BAM?

Solana stands alone: the only blockchain where trading SOL is cheaper and faster on-chain than on centralized exchanges. With BAM, that lead widens as apps gain the tools to outprice and out-execute other exchanges. BAM provides the execution infrastructure necessary to make on-chain trading not just competitive with centralized venues, but categorically superior.

The Block Assembly Marketplace (BAM), which Jito Labs revealed in July, represents the evolution toward a more transparent and application-driven transaction processing paradigm. BAM addresses a fundamental challenge that has constrained the network's growth: the inability for applications to provide predictable, high-quality execution guarantees to their users.

# Why Solana's Block Building Needed to Evolve

We’ve detected at least seven different transaction schedulers operating on Solana, each with distinct ordering logic and timing characteristics. This fragmentation creates fundamental uncertainty for applications and traders who depend on predictable execution. Traders cannot determine whether they're receiving fair execution or being systematically disadvantaged by opaque scheduling decisions made by validators with competing interests. The result is wider spreads and reduced liquidity as market makers price in execution uncertainty, while developers face inconsistent environments that undermine their ability to compete with centralized alternatives.

# Why Jito Labs Is Building BAM

Jito Labs helped pioneer trading infrastructure on Solana through our Block Engine. While these systems created significant value for validators and improved transaction execution, we've also watched how they've evolved. We've seen concentration dynamics emerge, observed the pressure toward private orderflow relationships, and recognized that without intervention, Solana risks following the same path toward opacity that degraded Ethereum's execution layer.

Ethereum's experience with proposer-builder separation (PBS) offers particularly instructive lessons. While PBS introduced competitive block building, it created perverse incentives: builders maximize MEV extraction to win block auctions, often at users' expense. This dynamic leaves the network stuck in a positive feedback loop that can be hard to unwind. Increased sandwich attacks and other forms of extractive MEV pushed orderflow private, as users sought protection through direct builder relationships. Builders offered rebates for exclusive access to this orderflow, and those with the most private flow could pay higher auction fees, winning more blocks and attracting even more exclusive deals. The result is a concentration of power among a small number of builders, increased opacity in transaction processing, and timing games that degraded network performance. Most concerningly, some dominant builders were operated by trading firms with direct financial incentives to exploit

sources.title: Introducing Drift v3: Built to Outperform  | Drift Updates
sources.snippet:
Introducing Drift v3: Built to Outperform  | Drift Updates
[![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6310e7dee49f0866da8eed4c/6867a1f64ca2c75f0d14f371_16c97ce9017b907297827c5113a068f9_Frame%201597880158.avif)](https://www.drift.trade/)
[
Launch App
](#)
## Introducing Drift v3: Built to Outperform
December 4, 2025
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6310e7dee49f08e5798eed50/693023e13e4b72ada17e0fe2_2025-12-03%2017.19.13.jpg)
[
Contents
](#)
[
Contents
](#)
[
](https://twitter.com/driftprotocol)[
](https://discord.com/invite/fMcZBH8ErM)
[
Trade Now
](https://app.drift.trade/)
![]()
![]()
Drift v3 is the protocol’s biggest performance upgrade yet. Rebuilt around trader execution, v3 delivers**10x faster fills, 10x improved liquidity, and a cleaner, more intuitive interface**than v2.
This upgrade was built for one thing: outperformance. Instant execution. Deeper liquidity. A suite of tools that helps you trade faster and with more confidence.
Drift v2 was the first to bring cross-margin and multi-asset collateral to DeFi on-chain trading. We built on Solana to deliver NASDAQ-level performance on-chain and with major Solana upgrades like Alpenglow, Firedancer and Jito’s Block Assembly Market (BAM), Drift is built to outperform as the network scales.
Since launch, Drift has surpassed 1.5B in TVL, $133B+ in total trading volume and $650M in open interest. Drift v3 is built to take those numbers and your trading even further.
**Drift v3 will be available on Dec 4, 2025.**New users will need an invite code to access Drift v3.
**DM**[**@DriftProtocol**](https://x.com/DriftProtocol)**to request access.**
## Why v3?
Drift v3 represents the protocol’s next stage of maturity: the point where years of infrastructure, liquidity, and execution improvements converge into a faster, clearer, more reliable trading experience.
**Over the past year, Drift has evolved in three critical areas:**
1. Execution speed
2. Liquidity depth
3. Trader experience## How Drift v3 outperforms
*The road to v3 was focused on improving liquidity, trading execution and speed. Here’s what that looks like in practice:*
### **Trading Execution and Speed**
* **10x faster order fills:**On Drift v3, 85% of market orders are filled within the same slot. A slot is Solana’s smallest time unit (about 400 milliseconds). On Drift, that means that trades are executed in under half a second. Instant execution.
* **15x faster take profit/stop loss triggers:**For BTC, it dropped from 15 slots (\~6 seconds) to 1 slot (\~0.4 seconds). This means your risk controls and exits now trigger immediately, reducing slippage and missed opportunities.
* **Gasless trading by default:**Unlike other DEXs, where you pay gas on every trade, Drift abstracts gas fees so that’s one less thing to worry about eating into profits
* **400ms oracle updates**: Prices update every 400 millisecond (roughly 2.5 times per second). This keeps quotes accurate and reduces the chance of stale pricing.
* **Scales with Solana:**As Solana increases block size and throughput, Drift automatically gets faster too, handling more trades per second.### **Liquidity**
* **10x reduction in slippage on market orders:**In Drift v2, large market orders could move the price by 20 bps. In v3, that’s been cut to just 2 bps. Traders now get far better pricing, especially on size.
* **5x tighter TP/SL execution:**Take-profit and stop-loss orders are far more precise. On major markets like SOL and BTC, fills are 4x tighter, while non-majors see up to 5x improvement. This means exits trigger closer to the price you set, reducing losses and protecting gains.
* **Introduction of Drift Liquidity Provider (DLP)**: Provides liquidity for both Perps and Spot markets. Community members can deposit funds into DLP to support market-making and earn a share of the pool's profits. Currently in the testing stage. Public launch in Q1 2026.
* **Expanded Market Maker Program:**With v3’s expanded market maker initiatives, long-tail altcoins now hav

tags.impact_reason: BAM by Jito introduces significant enhancements to Solana's infrastructure, enabling customizable and verifiable block scheduling, potentially influencing MEV and application-specific execution strategies.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a 'Modular Crystalline Engine' as the Semantic Anchor because the transcript describes BAM (Block Assembly Marketplace) as programmable, customizable infrastructure that schedules and assembles transactions into blocks. The modularity of the engine represents the 'plugins' and 'customizable sequencing rules', while the crystalline, transparent material speaks to the core principles of being 'transparent' and 'verifiable'. I included the diegetic text 'ACE' glowing on a core module, as Application-Controlled Execution is the key technological concept being operationalized.
visual_idea.description: A sophisticated, modular engine made of polished obsidian is assembling itself in a dark, high-tech environment. Translucent, crystalline blocks, etched with glowing blue circuit-like patterns, move with perfect precision, slotting into the central obsidian core. One of the key crystalline blocks being inserted has the letters 'ACE' glowing brightly from within its structure. Thin beams of light, representing transaction data, are being captured and perfectly ordered by the engine's assembly process. The background is a vast, out-of-focus server farm, creating a sense of immense scale and deep infrastructure.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A monumental, sophisticated, modular engine, meticulously crafted from highly reflective, polished obsidian, is in the process of dynamic self-assembly within a profoundly dark, high-tech environment. Translucent crystalline blocks, each intricately etched with vibrant, volumetric neon blue circuit-like patterns, glide with perfect, robotic precision, preparing to slot into the central, monolithic obsidian core. One specific, crucial crystalline block, caught mid-insertion, vividly displays the letters 'ACE' glowing brightly and volumetrically from deep within its crystalline structure, casting subtle light onto the surrounding obsidian. Delicate, thin beams of pure light, representing intricate transaction data, are actively being captured, sorted, and perfectly ordered by the engine's complex assembly process, forming luminous pathways. The background reveals a vast, sprawling server farm, rendered in an atmospheric, out-of-focus blur, stretching upwards and outwards, creating an overwhelming sense of immense scale, profound infrastructure, and verticality, with ample negative space above the engine to emphasize its epic height. The scene features photorealistic lighting with subtle depth-of-field to enhance focus on the main subject. The polished obsidian surfaces catch dramatic reflections, while the crystalline elements are illuminated internally by the neon glow. This scene is composed for a vertical 3:4 aspect ratio. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, global illumination, physically based rendering (PBR).

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a 'Modular Crystalline Engine' as the Semantic Anchor because the transcript describes BAM (Block Assembly Marketplace) as programmable, customizable infrastructure that schedules and assembles transactions into blocks. The modularity of the engine represents the 'plugins' and 'customizable sequencing rules', while the crystalline, transparent material speaks to the core principles of being 'transparent' and 'verifiable'. I included the diegetic text 'ACE' glowing on a core module, as Application-Controlled Execution is the key technological concept being operationalized.
visual_idea.description: A macro shot focusing on a single, translucent crystalline block locking into the polished obsidian core of a larger machine. Intricate, glowing blue circuits inside the crystal align perfectly with connection points on the obsidian. The interplay of light from the crystal reflects on the dark, mirror-like surface of the core, emphasizing the precision and high-end materials of the mechanism. The focus is on the satisfying 'click' of connection and perfect alignment.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A macro shot, 1:1 aspect ratio, perfectly centered, showing a single, translucent crystalline block precisely locking into the polished obsidian core of a larger, futuristic machine. The crystalline block is geometric and faceted, capturing and refracting light. Inside the crystal, intricate, glowing volumetric blue neon circuits pulse with a soft, ethereal light, aligning flawlessly with precise connection points on the dark, mirror-like surface of the polished obsidian core. The brilliant blue light from the crystal casts dramatic, vibrant reflections and subtle ambient occlusion across the obsidian, highlighting its high-shine finish and emphasizing the unparalleled precision and high-end materials of the mechanism. Focus is on the satisfying 'click' of connection and perfect alignment, with a deep depth of field to ensure sharp detail on both connecting parts. The entire frame is filled with the intricate textures of crystal and obsidian, creating a visually dense and luxurious composition. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, photorealistic, cinematic lighting, high dynamic range, physically based rendering (PBR).

editorial.headline: Jito Operationalizes 'ACE' Market Structure on 8% of Solana Stake
editorial.standfirst: Jito Labs is redefining Solana's execution layer by transitioning from generic high-throughput to verifiable, programmable capital markets. The introduction of Application-Controlled Execution (ACE) allows protocols to dictate their own ordering rules, a critical evolution for institutional liquidity.
key_takeaways: **Adoption Milestone:** Jito's Block Assembly Marketplace (BAM) is live on **8% of network stake**, processing hundreds of millions of transactions via Trusted Execution Environments.
**Programmable Liquidity:** The new **ACE** feature enables apps to enforce custom microstructure, such as maker priority, to tighten spreads and improve execution quality.
**Technical Roadmap:** Jito confirmed the development of **FireBAM**, a high-performance integration with the **Firedancer** client, alongside a full open-source release targeting Q1.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
The narrative at Breakpoint 2025 has shifted decisively from raw speed to execution determinism. **Jito Labs** CEO Lucas Bruder argued that while Solana has won the "speed war"—citing a 6x increase in TPS and exponential blockspace growth—the next frontier is the "structure war." The introduction of the **Block Assembly Marketplace (BAM)** represents a pivot toward a transparent, verifiable execution layer where the rules of the market are as immutable as the ledger itself. For institutional investors, this moves Solana from a high-speed casino to a regulated capital market venue.

The most significant alpha for developers and liquidity providers is **Application-Controlled Execution (ACE)**. Historically, on-chain applications have been beholden to generic, global transaction schedulers that often leak value to predatory MEV strategies. **ACE** inverts this model, allowing protocols to define their own sequencing logic. By implementing rules like "maker priority," DEXs can protect liquidity providers from toxic flow, resulting in tighter spreads and deeper liquidity. This capability allows Solana applications to mimic the sophisticated market microstructure of centralized high-frequency trading venues while retaining non-custodial security.

Crucially, **Jito** is addressing the "black box" problem that keeps many institutions on the sidelines. Through the use of Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs) and the upcoming **BAM Attestation Report Service (BARS)**, the protocol offers cryptographic proof that execution rules were followed. This verifiability—coupled with the announcement of **FireBAM**, a highly anticipated integration with the **Firedancer** client—suggests a future where high-performance, auditable execution is the standard, not the exception, for Solana's financial stack.
